items.title: Serena (knee) pulls out of Wimbledon doubles
items.summary: Serena Williams, the 23-time Grand Slam singles champion, has withdrawn from doubles competition at Wimbledon with a right knee injury, she announced Saturday.

items.title: Canada's historic FIFA World Cup run ends as Morocco march into quarterfinals
items.summary: Azzedine Ounahi gave Morocco the lead in the 50th minute and added another in the 82nd minute on a breakout before Soufiane Rahimi scored again.

items.title: Fritz last American man standing at Wimbledon
items.summary: Taylor Fritz became the last American man standing at Wimbledon on Saturday after he moved on to the final 16. He will face Alexander Bublik.

items.title: Mertens upsets Rybakina in Wimbledon 3rd round
items.summary: Iga Swiatek's title defense at Wimbledon ended in a 7-6 (9), 6-2 third-round loss to 21-year-old Alexandra Eala, who continues to make history for the Philippines.

items.title: Djokovic ties Federer with 105th Wimbledon win
items.summary: Novak Djokovic defeated Arthur Rinderknech on Centre Court for his 105th match win at Wimbledon, tying him with Roger Federer on the men's list for most singles match victories.
